description: generate synthetic MR NIfTI volumes via NV-Generate-CTMR rflow-mr (GPU)
rules: thin wrapper: inference, sampling, and decoding delegate entirely to upstream scripts.diff_model_infer — do not reimplement
use the documented entrypoint; never hand-write inference code or invent generate.sh/infer.py commands
do not modify code under $NV_GENERATE_ROOT or the repo-local fallback at .workbench_data/upstreams/NV-Generate-CTMR
MODALITY must be one of mri, mri_t1, mri_t2, mri_flair
outputs (NIfTI volumes + result JSON) are written under record_store; the wrapper creates its --output-dir
usage: Set MODEL_CONFIG (override JSON path or 'default'), MODALITY, and RANDOM_SEED. Requires NV_GENERATE_ROOT (clone of NV-Generate-CTMR) + downloaded rflow-mr weights + an NVIDIA GPU. Output: result.json (the wrapper summary) plus generated *.nii.gz volumes in record_store.
limitation: Thin wrapper around NV-Generate-CTMR's scripts.diff_model_infer. rflow-mr is image-only — no paired segmentation masks. Needs an NVIDIA GPU with >=16 GB VRAM; no CPU fallback. Weights are NVIDIA Non-Commercial; outputs are synthetic and not safe as production training data without review. Not for clinical deployment, interpretation, autonomous diagnosis, or regulatory submission. For brain MRI use nv-generate-mr-brain.
